title: 3Sum
problem_statement: Find all unique triplets in the array which give the sum of zero.

examples.explanation: Two unique triplets sum to zero.

intuition: Sort the array and use two pointers for the inner search to avoid duplicates.

approaches.name: Sort + two pointers
approaches.description: Fix one number and use two pointers to find pairs that sum to the negative of that number, while skipping duplicates.
approaches.why_not: Naive triple loops are O(n^3) and too slow.
interview_commentary: Sort the array first so the two-pointer scan can move inward predictably.
Skip duplicate values for both the fixed number and the moving pointers to avoid repeated triplets.
When the sum is too small, advance the left pointer; when too large, retreat the right pointer.

interview_qa.q: Why sort before using two pointers?
interview_qa.a: Sorting allows two pointers to move predictably and skip duplicates easily.

interview_qa.q: How do you avoid duplicate triplets?
interview_qa.a: Skip equal elements when fixing the first number and after finding a valid pair.
